provenance: Schema-derived from the autotune_runs table (database.py save_autotune_run signature; the selection_tstat column added by migration 014, replacing the pre-D3 deflated_sharpe column). Re-pinned for Decision D3: the autotuner selection-bias correction is now the Harvey & Liu 2015 multiple-testing haircut, and the persisted statistic is the winning trial's selection t-statistic (Sortino*sqrt(T)) — higher-is-better — NOT a Deflated Sharpe Ratio. naive_sharpe is the raw Optuna best Sortino; frozen_eval_sharpe is the withheld-fold Sortino. selection_tstat here is derived as naive_sharpe * sqrt(T) for an illustrative T=60 in-sample observation count: 1.8432 * sqrt(60) = 14.2772 (rounded to 4dp) — a realistic haircut t-stat, larger than the Sortino it is scaled from. Values are non-trivially distinct so formatting assertions can tell all three fields apart.
description: Autotune run row where all three surfacing fields are present: naive_sharpe (raw Optuna best Sortino), selection_tstat (Harvey & Liu haircut winner's Sortino*sqrt(T) — higher-is-better), frozen_eval_sharpe (honest post-selection metric on the withheld fold). selection_tstat > naive_sharpe here because the t-stat scales the Sortino by sqrt(T).
